steps.title: Distribute the multiplier
steps.content:
First distribute $6$ through the second parentheses. This changes the added expression into two terms:
$$
6(x^2+9)=6x^2+54
$$

steps.title: Rewrite the full expression
steps.content:
Now substitute the distributed terms back into the original sum:
$$
(x^3+8x^2-7x)+6x^2+54
$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Only the $x^2$ terms combine. The $x^3$ term, the $x$ term, and the constant term stay in the expression: $$ x^3+8x^2-7x+6x^2+54 $$ $$ x^3+14x^2-7x+54 $$ The answer is A.
